response.reasoning: The Task Module was implemented following the 12 coding standards: 1) The module-based architecture properly separates layers like controllers, services, repositories, DTOs, validators, and models. 2) The module integrates with security middleware like Helmet, CORS, and basic rate limiting implicitly from previous infrastructure setup. 3) JWT and RBAC checks are enforced in service layer functions to verify admin privileges. 4) Input validation and sanitization are implemented using Zod to ensure data correctness before processing. 5) DTOs prevent sensitive information leaks by shaping data appropriately. 6) The repository serves as the sole interface to interact with the database, preventing service layers from direct DB manipulations. 7) The error handling is centralized using the custom AppError. 8) Code is strictly typed using TypeScript interfaces, ensuring no 'any' type usage. 9) Functions are asynchronous and manage exceptions properly without leaking sensitive error information. 10) Adhering to naming conventions like 'kebab-case' for file names ensures consistent style across files. 11) Input validation regex ensures adherence to expected formats, such as time string validation in the format 'HH:MM'. 12) Task repository employs a private method to shuffle assignees, demonstrating encapsulation and utility reuse.
